paper.title: Glycolipid nanoparticles target the spleen and detarget the liver without charge
paper.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

formulations.targeting_details: Endogenous targeting via glycolipid-mediated protein corona change. Charge-independent liver de-targeting. C16GalphaCer replaces DSPC as helper lipid.
formulations.formulation_notes: GlycoLNP61 is the lead particle from 109-LNP barcoded screen. 8 molar ratio combinations tested per ionizable lipid. Exact mol% not reported in accessible text. Encapsulation efficiency 58%. Formed blebbed morphology vs smooth control.

formulations.targeting_details: Matched control with DSPC instead of C16GalphaCer. Identical molar ratios otherwise.
formulations.formulation_notes: Control particle for GlycoLNP61 comparison. Encapsulation efficiency 76%. Standard smooth LNP morphology.

results.liver_metric: Thy1.1+ cells in liver endothelial cells, Kupffer cells, hepatocytes, immune cells
results.liver_notes: SM-102 library: splenic F480+ delivery HIGHER than liver endothelial cells. cKK-E12 library: splenic F480+ delivery EQUAL to liver endothelial cells.

experiments.notes: Pooled barcoded screen. C16GalphaCer LNPs colocalized with Thy1.1+ splenic cells. C8GalphaCer did not. 25 cell types measured across liver, spleen, blood, lymph nodes. BM was NOT measured.

results.liver_notes: GlycoLNP61 showed higher Thy1.1+ cells in spleen than liver. Significant difference in spleen:liver ratio vs ControlLNP61. Neither particle delivered efficiently to other tested tissues.

experiments.notes: Individual validation confirmed screen results. Dose-response showed splenic delivery at doses as low as 0.2 mg/kg. scRNA-seq on 18,585 cells confirmed delivery to splenic cell types.

key_findings_for_project: Charge-INDEPENDENT liver de-targeting: replacing DSPC with neutral glycolipid C16GalphaCer shifts tropism from liver to spleen
No antibodies or permanent charge required - potentially safer than DOTAP-based approaches (avoids embolism risk at 40:1 ratio)
109 LNPs screened with DNA barcoding - large dataset for ML
Two ionizable lipids tested (SM-102 and cKK-E12) - generalizable principle
BM was NOT measured - critical gap for our project
Splenic macrophages (F480+) are primary target - not directly relevant to HSC but mechanism could inform BM de-targeting strategies
NKT cell engagement mentioned as possible mechanism for glycolipid-mediated tropism change

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Completely different approach (liver de-targeting vs HSC targeting). Complementary strategy.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_shi: No antibody targeting. Different goal (spleen enrichment vs HSC targeting).
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Dahlman lab (same as Kim). Uses SM-102 and cKK-E12 instead of PPZ-A10. Compatible barcoding methodology.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: No BM data so cannot place on Pareto. Liver de-targeting quantified but only as spleen:liver ratio, not absolute %.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: Demonstrates liver de-targeting principle without charge - relevant if combined with BM-targeting strategy.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: High relevance as novel mechanism for liver de-targeting. If combined with BM-targeting (e.g., CD117 antibody), could improve therapeutic window. SM-102 and cKK-E12 are known lipids with SMILES.

limitations_and_caveats: BM was NOT measured in any experiment - cannot assess HSC delivery
Spleen-focused delivery: may or may not translate to BM delivery
Exact molar ratios of GlycoLNP61 not extracted (8 ratio permutations tested)
Low encapsulation efficiency (58%) may limit therapeutic utility
Size difference between GlycoLNP61 (115nm) and control (60nm) confounds direct comparison
Only reporter readout (Thy1.1), no genome editing cargo tested
C16GalphaCer may engage NKT cells - potential immunogenicity concern for repeat dosing
